content.full_text:
June 27, 1990

Adrienne P. Unger  
General Attorney  
Corporate & Securities Law  
Shell Oil Company  
P.O. Box 2463  
Houston, TX 77252

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-90-391

Dear Ms. Unger:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

Your letter correctly summarizes the staff's advice that Shell Oil Company may make contributions in excess of the contribution limits to the Senate Republican Political Action Committee which will be used for administrative and overhead expenses and to repay debts incurred prior to January 1, 1989, some of which were incurred in connection with making contributions to candidates.

Please note that this advice is limited to contributions made to legislative caucus committees established prior to January 1, 1989, like the Senate Republican Political Action Committee, which are attempting to pay debts in order to terminate. Contributions to any other type of committee for the purpose of paying debts

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

incurred in connection with making contributions to candidates will most likely be subject to the Act's contribution limits.

Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional questions.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[Signature]
By: Carla Wardlow
Assistant Division Chief
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
